scope_note: QA-KG Determinism Cert [228] v1: Phase 5 determinism contract. Primary source: docs/specs/QA_MEM_SCOPE.md (Dale, 2026); tools/qa_kg/canonicalize.py (Dale, 2026); tools/qa_kg/fixtures/corpus_snapshot_v1/CANONICAL_MANIFEST.json (Dale, 2026); memory/project_qa_mem_review_role.md (Dale, 2026). Validates that the QA-KG build pipeline produces byte-stable canonical output given a frozen corpus fixture, and that the live production graph_hash is recorded in _meta_ledger.json for kg.promote() D6 staleness enforcement. Canonical serialization excludes build-time timestamps (created_ts/updated_ts/vetted_ts/last_check_*) and NFC-normalizes unicode; nodes ordered by id, edges ordered by (src_id,dst_id,edge_type), meta whitelisted to {schema_version}. Gates: (D1, HARD) fixture content hash matches expected_hash.fixture_hash; (D1.5, WARN) manifest.repo_head drift vs current HEAD; (D2, HARD) in-process rebuild twice gives byte-identical canonical hash; (D3, HARD) subprocess rebuild gives the same hash as D2; (D4, SHOULD or N-A) cross-platform scaffold — PASS only when tested on a non-Linux host; (D5, HARD) ledger graph_hash matches live production graph_hash (bootstrap PASS on first run with explicit rationale, compare PASS thereafter); (D6, HARD) kg.promote() enforces graph_hash staleness — ephemeral test mutating qa_kg.db asserts FirewallViolation; (D7, HARD) no `except Exception: pass` / bare `except: pass` swallows in tools/qa_kg/canonicalize.py or the validator (AST scan).
